description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Sign up for an in‑person adult study for ages around 18–30 that lasts no more than 90 minutes, pays at least $25, has sessions scheduled next week, and choose the earliest available weekday time slot starting at or after 10:00 AM before submitting the sign-up form with your details.

target_names: Emotions and Decision-Making in Young Adults

tasks.instruction: Among the lab PI’s developmental psychology publications from 2020–2023, add the three most recent ones to your reading list and then open that list.

tasks.instruction: Complete and submit a research assistant application selecting emotion regulation as your primary research interest along with exactly one additional area, indicating availability for at least two consecutive semesters and around 10 hours per week, and providing your academic and contact information.

expected_values.motivation_text: I am interested in gaining hands-on experience in emotion regulation research and learning more about experimental design.

tasks.instruction: From the lab members directory, find a graduate student whose research involves both sleep and memory, open their profile, and send a message through the site contact form indicating you are a potential participant interested in an adult sleep study.

expected_values.message_text: I would like to learn more about your sleep study for adults aged 1825 and how I can participate.
